rationale: A0 shows mimikatz.exe (executed directly from Administrator's Downloads folder) opening lsass.exe with access rights 0x1010 (PROCESS_QUERY_LIMITED_INFORMATION | PROCESS_VM_READ), a classic credential dumping pattern. EV-002 shows rundll32.exe accessing lsass.exe via comsvcs.dll (MiniDump technique, granted_access 0x1410), a well-known living-off-the-land credential dumping method. EV-003 shows xordump.exe from C:\Windows\Temp accessing lsass.exe with full access rights (0x1fffff) using dbgcore.dll, consistent with a memory dump tool designed to evade detection. Three distinct credential dumping techniques were executed against lsass.exe within approximately four minutes on the same host, indicating an active, hands-on-keyboard intrusion with credential harvesting as the primary objective.
